channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Tennessee teens sue Elon Musk's xAI over AI-generated child sexual abuse material
item.description: The three girls say that the nonconsensual nude images were created by a perpetrator who used AI company xAI's image generation tools.
item.content:encoded:
The three girls say that the nonconsensual nude images were created by a perpetrator who used AI company xAI's image generation tools.

(Image credit: Nicolas Tucat)
item.dc:creator: Huo Jingnan

item.title: Supreme Court to hear expedited arguments on protected status for migrants
item.description: The court temporarily blocked the Trump administration from deporting some 6,000 Syrians and 350,000 Haitians who were granted Temporary Protected Status.
item.content:encoded:
The court temporarily blocked the Trump administration from deporting some 6,000 Syrians and 350,000 Haitians who were granted Temporary Protected Status.

(Image credit: Kevin Dietsch)
item.dc:creator: Zoe Sobel

item.title: Here are the names of the 6 Air Force airmen who died when their refueling plane crashed
item.description: On Saturday, the U.S. Defense Department released the names of six service members who died when their military refueling aircraft crashed.
item.content:encoded: On Saturday, the U.S. Defense Department released the names of six service members who died when their military refueling aircraft crashed.
item.dc:creator: Hosts

item.title: How a gift of $50 was life-changing for this daughter of a Miami taxi driver
item.description: A daughter tells a story about her father, a taxi driver in Miami, who talked up her ambition to a repeat customer and got $50 specifically for her -- just enough for a life-changing opportunity.
item.content:encoded: A daughter tells a story about her father, a taxi driver in Miami, who talked up her ambition to a repeat customer and got $50 specifically for her -- just enough for a life-changing opportunity.
item.dc:creator: Jason Fuller

item.title: A new tool is helping to annihilate dangerous PFAS in firefighting foam
item.description: For decades, firefighters used a foam that contained PFAS, or forever chemicals, that can cause cancer and other illnesses. Now a "PFAS Annihilator" can destroy the toxic chemical in the foam.
item.content:encoded: For decades, firefighters used a foam that contained PFAS, or forever chemicals, that can cause cancer and other illnesses. Now a "PFAS Annihilator" can destroy the toxic chemical in the foam.
item.dc:creator: Adriana Martinez-Smiley

item.title: Why ICE agents are wearing body armor, helmets and camouflage clothing
item.description: Federal immigration agents are dressing like elite military special operators. Some say there are reasons for all that camouflage that go well beyond finding and arresting undocumented immigrants.
item.content:encoded: Federal immigration agents are dressing like elite military special operators. Some say there are reasons for all that camouflage that go well beyond finding and arresting undocumented immigrants.
item.dc:creator: Jay Price

item.title: In Alaska, wildlife managers are killing grizzlies in an attempt to save caribou
item.description: NPR's Ailsa Chang talks with Max Graham, a writer for High Country News, about Alaska's declining caribou population, and the state's plan to save them by shooting predators like grizzlies and wolves.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ailsa Chang talks with Max Graham, a writer for High Country News, about Alaska's declining caribou population, and the state's plan to save them by shooting predators like grizzlies and wolves.
item.dc:creator: Ailsa Chang

item.title: Team USA won the second-most medals at these Paralympics. See the standout moments
item.description: A mix of decorated veterans and rising stars won 24 medals for Team USA, 13 of them gold. The last one arrived Sunday, when the U.S. sled hockey team beat Canada to win its fifth straight gold medal.
item.content:encoded:
A mix of decorated veterans and rising stars won 24 medals for Team USA, 13 of them gold. The last one arrived Sunday, when the U.S. sled hockey team beat Canada to win its fifth straight gold medal.

(Image credit: Stefano Rellandini)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Trump demands NATO and China police the Strait of Hormuz. So far they aren't joining
item.description: With the Iran war entering a third week, Israel said it plans for at least three more weeks of war, while President Trump demanded other countries help the U.S. secure the vital Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded:
With the Iran war entering a third week, Israel said it plans for at least three more weeks of war, while President Trump demanded other countries help the U.S. secure the vital Strait of Hormuz.

(Image credit: Altaf Qadri)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Selection Sunday: Duke No. 1 overall in men's tournament, UConn women get top seed
item.description: Duke will be the top overall seed in the men's NCAA basketball tournament. In the women's, the top-ranked UConn Huskies are undefeated and hope to repeat as champions for the first time in a decade.
item.content:encoded:
Duke will be the top overall seed in the men's NCAA basketball tournament. In the women's, the top-ranked UConn Huskies are undefeated and hope to repeat as champions for the first time in a decade.

(Image credit: Jacob Kupferman)
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan
